title: Finite weighted P0 residuals have zero weighted mass

source.citation: S. C. Brenner and L. R. Scott, The Mathematical Theory of Finite Element Methods, 3rd ed., Springer, 2008, Ch. 4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: For any finite cell set with nonzero total mass, the mass-weighted residual from the weighted P0 average has zero total mass.
assumptions: s is a finite index set
u is a cell value function
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted residual has zero weighted sum.

declarations.name: weightedP0Average
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Average {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) : Real := (∑ i ∈ s, m i * u i) / (∑ i ∈ s, m i)

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Residual {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - weightedP0Average s m u

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual_mass_zero
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Residual_mass_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : ∑ i ∈ s, m i * weightedP0Residual s m u i = 0

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index weighted P0 projection fact `weightedP0Residual_mass_zero` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Residual_mass_zero {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (hmass : (∑ i ∈ s, m i) ≠ 0) : ∑ i ∈ s, m i * weightedP0Residual s m u i = 0
Unfold weightedP0Residual and weightedP0Average, distribute the finite sum, use Finset.sum_mul, and field_simp with the nonzero total mass.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or weighted-projection lemma needed for the proof.
